text.title: ab-testing Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for ab-testing as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Stopping the test early when results look favorable.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Peeking invalidates standard p-values; the full pre-calculated sample is required for valid inference.

failure-mode.description: Testing too many things simultaneously in one variant.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Multiple simultaneous changes make attribution impossible; you cannot know which change drove the result. Test one variable at a time.

failure-mode.description: Ignoring guardrail metrics when declaring a winner.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A variant can improve the primary metric while degrading revenue, session depth, or user trust; guardrails prevent shipping net-negative changes.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Undocumented null results cause teams to run the same tests repeatedly; every result has learning value.

failure-mode.description: Detecting segment-level wins without checking population-level significance.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: One segment winning while the full population is flat is not a ship signal; population-level significance is the primary gate. Segment analysis is secondary confirmation.
